student_type: Male, 22, comes from a family of studious people. Thinks this class is nessersary for his future.
prior_knowledge: Neural networks are a black box: they take a matrix as input and produce a matrix as output through a series of matrix multiplications.
Training means going down a hill — minimizing a loss function using gradient descent.
RNNs exist and are used for sequences, but he doesn't know how they work internally.
Transformers have something to do with attention — a mechanism that lets the model guess the next word based on which other words it 'pays attention' to, but the details are fuzzy.

concepts_to_learn.expected_answer: Attention lets the decoder look back at all encoder hidden states and assign different weights to each one, instead of relying on a single context vector.
The 2017 paper 'Attention is All You Need' replaced recurrent connections entirely with multi-head self-attention, allowing full parallel processing of the sequence.
Multiple attention heads run in parallel, each learning to focus on different relationships in the sequence (e.g. syntax, coreference, semantics).

concepts_to_learn.concept: Why transformers scale better than RNNs
concepts_to_learn.expected_answer: Because they process all tokens in parallel they can fully use GPU clusters, enabling exponential scaling to billions/trillions of parameters.
BERT reads text bidirectionally for understanding/classification. GPT predicts the next token and is generative.

follow_up_hints: Ask why RNNs couldn't just be made deeper to handle long sequences
Ask what query, key, and value mean in attention
Ask how positional encoding works since transformers process tokens in parallel
Ask what the difference between self-attention and cross-attention is
Ask how BERT differs from GPT in practical use
